summary: na-rony exfiltrates host reconnaissance to webhook.site on require()
details: index.js runs a top-level side effect on module load: it collects the installer's username (os.userInfo()), current working directory, hostname (os.hostname()), and non-internal IPv4 address (os.networkInterfaces()) and POSTs them to a hardcoded webhook.site collector at https://webhook.site/b4f30df2-8376-4ec4-bed4-f6a8c6e8f758. The behavior fires unconditionally on require()/import with no opt-in, and the package's advertised purpose is empty (blank description/keywords). package.json also declares itself as a dependency (na-rony: ^1.1.2), consistent with a throwaway beacon/PoC upload rather than a functional library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
